lessons.title: Entering & the Queue
lessons.scene: You and R reach a temple for evening darshan. At the entrance, an elderly volunteer manages footwear and the queue.

steps.speaker: Volunteer
steps.kannada: Banni, chappali illi bidi. Olage hogi.
steps.script: ಬನ್ನಿ, ಚಪ್ಪಲಿ ಇಲ್ಲಿ ಬಿಡಿ. ಒಳಗೆ ಹೋಗಿ.
steps.pronunciation: BAN-ni, CHAP-pa-li IL-li BI-di. O-la-ge HO-gi
steps.english: Come, leave your slippers here. Go inside.

steps.prompt: Ask whether you should leave your footwear here

choices.english: Should I leave the slippers here?
Should I bring the slippers inside?
choices.hint: Footwear is never taken inside a temple. Confirm leaving them here: 'illi bidbeka?'.
You're not buying slippers! Ask whether to leave them here: 'illi bidbeka?'.

correctReaction.speaker: Volunteer
correctReaction.english: Yes, leave them here. Stand in the queue.

steps.grammarTip: Turn '-beku' (must) into a question with '-aa': 'bidbeku' (must leave) → 'bidbeka?' (should I leave?). It's the polite way to check what you're expected to do — very handy in unfamiliar places.

steps.prompt: Ask the volunteer what time the darshan (deity viewing) is

choices.english: At what time is the darshan?

choices.hint: 'Doora' (far) is for distance. For time, ask 'eshtu gante-ge?' (at what o'clock?).
You want the timing, not who. Ask 'eshtu gante-ge?' (at what time?).

correctReaction.speaker: Volunteer
correctReaction.kannada: Aidu vare-ge bagilu tegitaare. Swalpa kaayi.
correctReaction.english: They open the doors at five-thirty. Wait a little.

steps.grammarTip: Half-past is 'vare': 'aidu-vare' = five-thirty (five and a half). Quarter-past is 'kaalu', quarter-to is 'mukkaalu'. Add '-ge' for 'at': 'aidu-vare-ge' = at five-thirty.

lessons.title: Archana & Prasada
lessons.scene: Inside, you reach the counter where the priest takes names for an archana (a personal offering with your name and star).

steps.speaker: Priest
steps.english: Do you want to have an archana done? Tell me the name and star (nakshatra).

steps.prompt: Say yes, and give your name as S

choices.hint: You came for the archana — that needs your name! Say 'houdu' and give it: 'hesaru S antha'.

choices.english: Whose name is it? / Who's the name?
choices.hint: He's asking YOU for the name. Just give it: 'hesaru S antha'.

correctReaction.speaker: Priest
correctReaction.kannada: S... nakshatra gottilla andre parvagilla. Munde banni.
correctReaction.script: ಎಸ್... ನಕ್ಷತ್ರ ಗೊತ್ತಿಲ್ಲ ಅಂದ್ರೆ ಪರವಾಗಿಲ್ಲ. ಮುಂದೆ ಬನ್ನಿ.
correctReaction.pronunciation: ES... NAK-sha-tra GOT-til-la an-dre par-VAA-gil-la. MUN-de BAN-ni
correctReaction.english: S... if you don't know the star, it's fine. Come forward.

steps.grammarTip: 'Gottilla' = 'I don't know (a fact)'. Different from 'baralla' = 'I can't / don't know how (a skill)'. So 'nakshatra gottilla' (I don't know the star) but 'kannada swalpa baratte' (I know a little Kannada).

steps.speaker: Priest
steps.kannada: Idu teertha, idu prasada. Kai munde maadi.
steps.script: ಇದು ತೀರ್ಥ, ಇದು ಪ್ರಸಾದ. ಕೈ ಮುಂದೆ ಮಾಡಿ.
steps.pronunciation: i-du TEER-tha, i-du pra-SAA-da. kai MUN-de MAA-di
steps.english: This is teertha (holy water), this is prasada. Hold your hand out.

steps.prompt: Receive it gratefully and thank him

choices.english: Thank you, swami. That was very good (of you).
No swami, I don't want prasada.
choices.hint: Refusing prasada is considered impolite at a temple. Accept it with a 'dhanyavada'.
Prasada isn't bought — it's received. Simply thank him: 'dhanyavada swami'.

correctReaction.speaker: Priest
correctReaction.english: May God do good (for you). May it be auspicious.

wrongReaction.speaker: Priest
wrongReaction.english: Take the prasada, it's God's blessing.

steps.grammarTip: Blessings use the gentle '-li' ending: 'aagli' (let it be), 'maadli' (may they do), 'shubhavaagli' (may it be auspicious). Receiving prasada with both hands and a 'dhanyavada' is the warm, respectful response.
